A technical breakdown of Microsoft AI’s new model family, with a deep dive into the reasoning model, the training loop, and Frontier Tuning.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Inside MAI-Thinking-1: How Microsoft Built a Reasoning Model From ScratchThis article breaks down how Microsoft AI built its new family of models, with most of the attention on MAI-Thinking-1, its first reasoning model. I will walk through the architecture, the data pipeline, the reinforcement learning loop that teaches the model to reason, the safety training, the benchmark results, and how Frontier Tuning lets you adapt the model on your own data. The goal is simple: by the end, you will understand the actual design choices behind these models and be able to reason clearly about how to use or tune them yourself.
Reference here.
The model family at a glance
Microsoft AI shipped seven models at once. They are not seven unrelated experiments; they share one training stack, one data lineage, and one infrastructure backbone. Here is the lineup.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Notice the pattern: most categories ship a maximum quality model alongside a Flash variant tuned for throughput and cost per…
